message:
refactor(runtime): replace resize debounce with ResizeCoalescer

The old resize handling had three behaviors (Immediate, Throttled,
Placeholder) implemented via a hand-rolled debounce timer that
accumulated pending resize events. The Placeholder mode drew a
decorative resize indicator but added complexity for little UX benefit.

This refactor:

- Removes the Placeholder resize variant entirely, simplifying
  ResizeBehavior to just Immediate and Throttled

- Replaces the ad-hoc debounce timer with the new ResizeCoalescer
  module, which provides configurable coalescing with proper
  continuous-reflow support

- Replaces resize_debounce Duration config with a CoalescerConfig
  struct that encapsulates coalescing parameters

- Removes ~110 lines of placeholder rendering logic (draw_resize_hint,
  ResizeState tracking, debounce timer management) that are no longer
  needed

Also in this commit:

- snapshot_player: add diagnostics_visible() accessor and update help
  text to document vim-style keybindings (h/l for step, g/G for
  first/last, D for diagnostics panel)

- no_flicker_proof: fix hex seed literals to use proper u64 formatting
  (0x000D_A00F_XXXX instead of 0xDA00F_XXXX)

- mpc_vs_pi_evaluation: new proptest comparing MPC vs PI controller
  strategies for resize frame pacing under various terminal size
  change patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
